events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. events.description: Gambit the Magician leads a magical performance to pique the interest of Families towards science! This fun and entertaining presentation reinforces STEM programs with discussions and magic tricks from each major branch of science. Discover the value of scientific education while also learning about the exciting benefits of science towards any career path! This is a Drop in program. Space is limited.

events.title: The Amazing Magic Mel
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as she leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fast-paced, comedy magic show for the entire family. Drop-in program. Space is limited. FREE tickets are available at 1:30pm. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Tor

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10am   to  10:30am .

events.title: Family Time & Stay and Play
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under with their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:00am to 10:30am.  Activity stations will be set up for self-directed learning and play from 10:30am to 11:00am. Participants will be capped at the first 25 families.

events.title: Little Builders
events.description: A fun construction zone where toddlers and preschoolers can play, stack, and create with giant building blocks. Ages 1-5. Drop-in.

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: BenTastic Magic Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! Register here or by calling 416-396-8939.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for event and program registration, delivery and evaluation. Questions about how the library handles your information can be directed to Answerline staff at answerline@tpl

events.title: Arts & Crafts
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: EarlyON Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 5 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment. This program is offered in partnership with the Malvern Family Resource Centre (MFRC) . This is a drop-in program. No registration required.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers.  Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 10:30 to 11:00am. Babies, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play until 11:30am.

events.title: Wednesday Whimsy
events.description: For children ages 2-6 with their parents or caregivers. Join us for Wednesday Whimsy! Little ones ages 2-6 will enjoy a brand-new adventure each time. We'll spark imagination and learning through stories, crafts, songs, and playful activities. Get ready to wiggle, giggle, and discover something wonderful! No registration – but space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Summer Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. For the months of July and August, weekday Family Time will take place on Wednesdays.  Toddler Time and Baby Time will resume in September.

events.title: The Magic Mel Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as Mel leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fun, fast-paced magic show for the entire family.  For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Board Games for Kids
events.description: Jenga! Monopoly! Ticket to Ride! And More! Spend the afternoon playing different board games with your friends!  Snacks are offered during this program for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: A Very Gru-Some Summer Movie Series
events.description: Experience the hit movies featuring supervillain turned secret agent, Gru and his group of colourful little minions this summer in our library's theatre! Movie will play at 2pm sharp! July 3 - Despicable Me. (2010) 95 min. Rated G  July 17 - Despicable Me 2. (2013) 98 min. Rated G July 31 - Despicable Me 3. (2017) 90 min. Rated G August 7 - Minions. (2015) 91 min. Rated G August 21 - Minions: The Rise of Gru. (2022) 88 min. Rated G Drop-in. No registration needed.

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: Family Book Chat
events.description: Drop in and share a favourite book or story you’ve read together. Read a passage, tell us about your favourite character or share something new you’ve discovered. Don’t forget about non – fiction, too! Relax and Chat!

events.title: Swallowing Clouds Workshop Series
events.description: Join professional artists in a fun and exciting community based arts program. This workshop offers an introduction into the enriching world of art and creativity through poetry, music, theatre and visual arts. Registration includes all four dates of this series (May 13, 20, 27 & June 3).  Ages 9-12 . Registration required.

events.title: After School Chess @ Armour Heights Library
events.description: New to chess? No stress! Join us after school to learn, play, and take on  fun chess challenges and activities! All are welcome – no registration required

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Every Wednesday we’ll get to explore robotics, circuitry, engineering, coding and art with a different activity each week! Wednesday, May 6: Pipe Cleaner Flowers Wednesday, May 13: Musical Dash Robots Wednesday, May 20: Colourful Abstract Painting Wednesday, May 27: Snap Circuits

events.title: Asian Heritage Month Storytime
events.description: Join us for a special storytime program to celebrate Asian Heritage Month. This interactive session features captivating stories and tales from Asia, followed by hands-on origami craft where kids can create paper cranes, flowers, or lanterns. All ages are welcome.

events.title: Fay & Fluffy's Story Time
events.description: Join Fay & Fluffy (stars of Family Jr’s The Fabulous Show with Fay & Fluffy) as they share some of their favourite books and stories. Fay & Fluffy’s main goal is to share the message of inclusivity wherever they go, cheering on all the kids who see them be proud of who they are, loving themselves, and being kind to one another. Drop-in. No registration required. Space is limited.

events.title: Fire Fighter Storytime
events.description: Join us for this fun story time to learn about fire safety and prevention, and what to do if there's an emergency. You will meet a Toronto Fire Fighter! At the end of the story time, you'll create a craft to take home. For children ages 0-5+ with a parent or caregiver. Drop in.  No Registration required.

events.title: Kamishibai: Storytelling & Craft
events.description: Join us for traditional Japanese Kamishibai storytelling told with illustrated cards, followed by a fun craft activity. For all ages! DROP-IN!

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: 3D Design with Tinkercad for Kids (Ages 9+)
events.description: Learn the basics of 3D design using a browser-based program called Tinkercad. You'll learn how to navigate in the 3D environment and create a simple object. Basic mouse and keyboarding skills are required. This course is designed for ages 9 - 14. R egistration is required.

events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve.

events.title: Saturday Family Club
events.description: Come celebrate your Saturday morning at Port Union Library! You can colour your favorite characters or create freestyle Legos with family and friends!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot. No registration required.

events.title: Girls Coding
events.description: This program introduces children to beginner computer programming and fosters the development of essential STEM skills. Delivered in collaboration with Macaulay Centres for Children, the initiative focuses on promoting educational growth and enhancing future employment opportunities, particularly for children in marginalized communities. Participants will engage in hands-on coding activities, make new friends, and have fun in a supportive. Space is limited, and advance registration with a commit

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday STEAM Club
events.description: Join us for Saturday STEAM Club for fun-filled Arts or STEM programs. Come explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through hands-on activities and creative projects. It's a fun, interactive way to learn and discover new skills in a supportive environment! Younger children, accompanied by a caregiver, are welcome if space permits. Program will run once every month.

events.title: Art Journaling
events.description: Join us for an hour of creative art journaling fun! An art journal can include writing, drawing, painting, collage, stickers and more! No experience necessary, all materials will be provided. This is a drop-in program for all ages, no registration required.

events.title: Story Time and Craft
events.description: Join us on Saturday afternoons at Kennedy/Eglinton for a Story Time and Craft. We will read stories, sing songs, do a craft and have fun! All children and their caregivers are welcome.

events.title: Mimico Kids & Parents Book Club
events.description: Our book for May is Grimm's Fairy Tales by Jakob & Wilhelm Grimm. First published in 1812, these timeless stories have enchanted generations of children. Some of the stories are scary, some whimsical, but all are spellbinding and have had a huge influence down through the years. These stories first introduced characters such as Hansel and Gretel, Rapunzel, Tom Thumb, and many more. Copies available at the library. Grimm's Fairy Tales

events.title: PA Day Movie: Zootopia 2
events.description: Rabbit detective Judy Hopps, and her partner Nick Wilde, a fox, must go undercover to help kind hearted Gary De'Snake clear his family name.  This 2025 film is rated PG and is recommended for children 8 and up. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.  Drop in.  Space is limited.

events.title: PA Day Family Movie: Zootopia 2
events.description: Join us this PA Day at the library for a screening of the family movie - Zootopia 2. Detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde find themselves on the twisting trail of a mysterious reptile who arrives in Zootopia and turns the mammal metropolis upside down. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before. Rated PG. 1hr 48 mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill

She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' events.title: Arts & Crafts

events.title: Puppet Show

events.title: Nature in the City Summer Camp
events.description: Toronto is full of life! Have fun learning about local urban wildlife through activities, games, and hands-on exploration. Discover birds, mammals, and even more amazing creatures that share the city with us! Presented by the Toronto and Region Conservation Authority. For children ages 7- 9 years old.

events.description: VEX GO Robotics Workshop introduces students to foundational concepts in engineering, robotics, and basic programming. Through guided, hands-on activities, learners complete several applied STEM projects, including: autonomous vehicle build, mechanical lift system, object transport robot and engineering structures.  This program is designed for kids ages 9 to 12 years old. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Board Games for Kids

events.title: How to Write Non-fiction
events.description: Writing nonfiction can be a challenge but author Erin Silver will make it fun and easy! We will discuss what nonfiction writing is, how we can make it sound more like fiction, and how to organize and write engaging A+ nonfiction! Erin will share insider tips about her process, where her ideas come from, and will also read from her nonfiction book, Rush Hour: Navigating Our Global Traffic Jam .

events.title: Pool Noodle Boats
events.description: Using pool noodles and straws, learn how to make a fun DIY water toy that you can race with your friends! All supplies will be provided. Drop-in, but space is limited.

events.title: Let's Make a Graphic Novel!
events.description: A workshop that teaches the fundamentals of story outlining and narrative structure as it applies to comics & graphic novels, prose, or even video games! Led by professional cartoonist, Tory Woollcott author of, “The Brain, The Ultimate Thinking Machine”. events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: Sunday SNAP Circuits
events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.title: Curious Chaos Club
events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.title: Sunday Tech Time
events.description: Explore the world of circuitry and robots. Test out Snap Circuits, Dash robots, or Spheros. Ages 5 and up.

events.title: Sunday Fun STEAM
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits,.. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in: No registration required.

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: STEAM Lab
events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Sunday STEAM at the Riverdale Branch
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits, LEGO, K'nex, Marble Run and Kids Craft Club. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. For children ages 6-12. Drop-in. Space and supplies are limited.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday LEGO
events.description: Do you love to build and create? Join us for LEGO Sundays and build something awesome! For children ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: STEAM Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to STEAM Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids, and have fun! events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. Games available for use in the Children's Area. Available games include: Ball Fall Jumbo (Ages 3+), STEM Pretend Flower Garden Toy Set (Ages 3-6), Operation (Ages 6+), STEM Road Builder Game (Ages 4-8), Dinosaur Tetra Tower Balance Stacking Blocks Game (Ages 6+) Available puzzles include: Giant World Map Puzzle (

events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers.

events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Sunday STEAM at the Riverdale Branch
events.description: Join us for a fun STEAM program on Sunday afternoon! Programs cycle between Robotics, Snap Circuits, LEGO, K'nex, Marble Run and Kids Craft Club. For Children ages 5 and up. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. For children ages 6-12. Drop-in. Space and supplies are limited.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Board Games
events.description: Join us for an afternoon of board game fun! Play different games and make new friends too! Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: STEM Programming For Children
events.description: Come and enjoy after school robotics and snap circuit programs!

events.title: Coding with Python III
events.description: Python is an open source programming language that is used for creating web applications, web content, mathematics and system scripting. In this follow up course we will really start to stretch our legs in Python, designing and implementing some simple Object Oriented Programming data structures. Key takeaways will include a working knowledge of Python classes and objects. This class is for beginners but assumes you have a basic understanding of Python or have attended Coding with Python I and I

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Photo Editing and Management with Adobe Lightroom Classic
events.description: Adobe Lightroom Classic is an industry standard tool used by photographers to organize, retouch, their work. We will focus on learning the workflow of the software, covering topics such as importing photos, file organization, retouching and correcting our images, and exporting and sharing our finished work. events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.description: This Drop-In demonstration is for patrons over the age of 13 years old. Leap into the world of virtual reality at the library! Join this drop-in session to try out the Meta Quest virtual reality headset and immerse yourself in stunning 360-degree environments, engage in captivating adventures, and interact with the virtual world.

events.title: All Ages Karaoke and Board Game Night
events.description: Come sing your favourite tunes on our stage in a fun, encouraging environment. Request any song and we'll try to find a karaoke video and play it for you! If singing is not your thing, a few board games will be on hand if you want to try out Catan, Anomia, Ticket to Ride, or test your vocabulary with Scrabble! You are also welcome to bring your own and invite others to play with you. Children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Chess Club

events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.title: Kimbap Making with Chef Sang
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of making Korean-style kimbap, exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. Participants will roll and cut kimbap using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of kimbap, its differences from the Japanese sushi roll, and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best kimbap creations. events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
